assumptions: The intragroup dividend has become a recognised intragroup monetary receivable/payable.
The foreign currency exposure arises because the entities involved have different functional currencies.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.
